steps.title: Use the parallel-line shortcut
steps.content: No solution means parallel distinct lines: same slope, different $y$-intercept. Put the given equation into Desmos as $$3x=45y-27 \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{15}+\frac{3}{5}$$ The second line must have slope $\frac{1}{15}$ but a different intercept.

steps.title: Graph the only matching-slope choice
steps.content: Choice B gives $$\frac{1}{3}x=5y \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{15}$$ This has the same slope, $\frac{1}{15}$, and a different intercept, $0$. In Desmos, $y=\frac{x}{15}+\frac{3}{5}$ and $y=\frac{x}{15}$ are parallel distinct lines, so they never meet.

steps.title: Eliminate the rest
steps.content: A has slope $\frac{1}{5}$, so it intersects the given line. C is exactly the same as the given line, so it has infinitely many solutions. D has slope $\frac{1}{45}$, so it intersects the given line. Therefore, the correct answer is choice B.
